title: Beach Surfing Trivia
description: 180 family-friendly questions about surfing, surfboards, and riding waves in Jeopardy format

questions.answer: Winter or any season depending on location

questions.question: What is the top side of a surfboard called?

questions.answer: California, Hawaii, Australia, or tropical regions

questions.question: What do surfers check before heading to the beach?

What is the name of the area where waves form regularly?

What is a 'barrel' in surfing?

questions.question: How do surfers move from lying down to standing on the board?
What are the fins on a surfboard designed to do?
What is the wave's steepest point called?
What should surfers wear to protect their skin from the sun?

What happens when the back of a wave closes out completely?

questions.answer: The whitewater after a wave breaks

What is the period between wave sets called?
What are the ridges on the bottom of a surfboard for?

What is the hollow part of a breaking wave called?
What is the term for waves that break onto a sandy beach?
What is the term for waves that break onto rocks?
What is the term for waves that break onto a rocky point?
What is the speed of a wave called?
What is the vertical distance from trough to crest called?
What is a 'lip' of a wave?
questions.answer: The very top edge of a breaking wave

questions.question: What do surfers use to carry their boards to the beach?
questions.answer: A board bag or under their arm

questions.question: What is the most common surfboard length in feet?
questions.answer: Six to seven feet or five to six feet

questions.question: What is the width measurement of a surfboard called?
What is the measurement from nose to tail called?
What is the thickness of a surfboard measured as?
What Hawaiian word means 'wave sliding'?
In what Hawaiian islands is surfing most popular?
What is the most famous surf spot in Hawaii?
What California beach is famous for surf?
What Australian location is known for great waves?
What is the term for a surfer who rides backwards?
questions.answer: Regular footer or goofy footer depending on direction

questions.question: What is a 'goofy' stance in surfing?
What is a 'regular' stance in surfing?
What is the foam core of a surfboard called?
What is the process of shaping a surfboard called?
What are the small fins at the back of some boards called?
What is the center fin of a three-fin setup called?
questions.answer: The main fin or center fin

questions.question: What is the back fin of a surfboard called?
What type of surfboard is best for large waves?
questions.answer: A gun or big wave board

questions.question: What type of board is best for small waves?
What is a 'fish' surfboard?
questions.answer: A short, wide board with a split tail

questions.question: What is a 'longboard'?
questions.answer: A board typically eight feet or longer

questions.question: What is a 'shortboard'?
questions.answer: A board typically under six feet

questions.question: What is a 'mini-mal' board?
questions.answer: A board between a shortboard and longboard

questions.question: What is a 'gun'?
questions.answer: A narrow board designed for big waves

questions.question: What is the most popular professional surfing competition?
questions.answer: The World Surf League or WSL Championship Tour

questions.question: What year was the World Surf League founded?
How many judges typically score a professional surfing heat?
What is the maximum wave height for beginners?
What is 'tidal bore' in surfing?
questions.answer: A wave formed by rising tide in a river

questions.question: What is a 'groundswell'?
questions.answer: Ocean swells produced by distant storms

questions.question: What is the scientific term for the sideways movement of water from a wave?
What causes a 'dumper' wave?
questions.answer: Shallow water or steep ocean floor

questions.question: What is the term for waves that haven't broken yet?
What is 'wind swell'?
questions.answer: Waves caused by local wind conditions

questions.question: What temperature range is ideal for surfing without a wetsuit?
What is the most common cause of rip currents?
questions.answer: Water pushed back through channels during breaking waves

questions.question: What should a surfer do if caught in a rip current?
What does PU stand for in surfboard construction?
What does EPS stand for in surfboard construction?
What is a 'soft top' surfboard made from?
What is the base of a surfboard's bottom called?
What does 'volume' refer to in surfboards?
What is a 'pintail' on a surfboard?
questions.answer: A pointed tail used for hold in bigger waves

questions.question: What is a 'rounded pin' tail?
questions.answer: A hybrid between pintail and rounded tail

questions.question: What is a 'swallow tail' on a surfboard?
questions.answer: A tail with a V-notch in the middle

questions.question: What measurement determines flotation in a surfboard?
What is the reference point for judging wave difficulty?
How much shorter is Hawaiian scale height than face height?
What is a 'closeout' in surfing?
questions.answer: When an entire section of wave breaks at once

questions.question: What is the area where surfers wait for waves called?
What is the path a surfer takes down a wave called?
What is a 'snap' in surfing?
What is a 'cutback' in surfing?
questions.answer: A sharp turn to redirect toward the wave face

questions.question: What is an 'air' in surfing?
questions.answer: When the surfer launches the board off the wave

questions.question: What is a 'maneuver' in surfing?
questions.answer: Any intentional action or trick on a wave

questions.question: What is the most important skill for beginners?
What is the recommended time to practice before catching waves?
questions.answer: Several sessions on land or in shallow water

questions.question: What is the core benefit of wetsuits in cold water?
What thickness of wetsuit is needed for 50-55 degree water?
What is the best time of day for offshore winds?
What is the Beaufort Scale used for in surfing forecasts?
questions.answer: Measuring wind speed and wave conditions

questions.question: What is the period of a wave measured in?
What do surfing forecasts use 'period' to indicate?
What is a 'fetch' in terms of wave generation?
questions.answer: Distance wind travels over ocean water

questions.question: What affects the angle at which waves approach the shore?
What is 'refraction' of waves?
questions.answer: Bending of waves as they encounter shallow water

questions.question: What is 'diffraction' of waves?
What is 'interference' in wave behavior?
questions.answer: When multiple waves combine to create larger or smaller waves

questions.question: What causes a 'standing wave' or 'stationary wave'?
questions.answer: Waves reflecting off obstacles in the same direction as incoming waves

questions.question: What is 'undertow'?
questions.answer: The return flow of water toward the ocean after waves push water ashore

questions.question: What is the relationship between water temperature and surfboard volume?
What is 'template' in surfboard design?
questions.answer: The outline shape of the board

questions.question: What is 'bottom contour' in surfboard design?
questions.answer: The three-dimensional shape of the board's bottom surface

questions.question: What is 'rail shape' in surfboards?
questions.answer: The thickness and curve of the board's edges

questions.question: What is the center of buoyancy in a surfboard?
questions.answer: The geometric center of the board's volume

questions.question: What is the center of gravity in relation to surfboard performance?
questions.answer: The balance point where the surfer stands

questions.question: What is 'planshape' in surfboard terminology?
questions.answer: The two-dimensional outline of the board from above

questions.question: What does 'swing weight' refer to in surfboards?
questions.answer: How responsive the board feels to turning

questions.question: What is 'drive' in surfboard performance?
questions.answer: Forward momentum and speed through turns

questions.question: What is 'hold' in surfboard performance?
questions.answer: The grip of the board in the wave face during turns

questions.question: What is 'pivot' in surfboard performance?
questions.answer: The ability to turn the board by rotating on its center

questions.question: What is the most dangerous aspect of big wave surfing?
questions.answer: Shallow water, sharp rocks, and hold-down durations

questions.question: What safety equipment do big wave surfers use?
questions.answer: Jet skis, spotters, and specialized boards

questions.question: What is a 'duck dive' in surfing?
questions.answer: A technique to roll under incoming waves while paddling

questions.question: What is an 'Eskimo roll' in surfing?
questions.answer: Rolling the board and paddling technique to get under waves

questions.question: What is 'tube riding' or 'barrel riding'?
questions.answer: Surfing through the hollow section of a breaking wave

questions.question: What is the most valuable skill in professional surfing scoring?
What is the scoring range in professional surfing competitions?
What is a 'heat' in professional surfing?
questions.answer: A competitive session where surfers battle for points

questions.question: What is the time limit for a professional surfing heat?
What is the theoretical maximum wave height in the ocean?
What is the deepwater wavelength formula based on?
What is the breaking wave height in shallow water?
What is 'shoaling' in wave physics?
questions.answer: The process of waves increasing in height as they approach shallow water

questions.question: What is the Coriolis effect's influence on wave patterns?
questions.answer: Deflection of wave direction based on Earth's rotation

questions.question: What is the relationship between swell period and wave speed?
What is 'dispersion' in swell behavior?
questions.answer: Separation of swell components by period as they travel

questions.question: What surfing legend pioneered tow-in surfing?
What was the first notable tow-in wave ridden?
questions.answer: Waves at Pipeline, Hawaii, in the 1990s

questions.question: What is the XXL Biggest Wave Award given for?
questions.answer: The largest wave surfed in a calendar year

questions.question: What is the most dangerous break in terms of hold-down duration?
What is the Mach number in surfing aerodynamics?
questions.answer: Ratio of board speed to wave speed

questions.question: What is the relationship between wetsuit thickness and freedom of movement?
questions.answer: Thicker suits reduce mobility but increase warmth

questions.question: What is 'laminar flow' in surfboard design?
questions.answer: Smooth water flow over the board surface

questions.question: What is 'turbulent flow' in surfboard hydrodynamics?
What is the CG (center of gravity) position for high-performance shortboards?
questions.answer: Forward of center for better control

questions.question: What is the CB (center of buoyancy) in relation to board balance?
questions.answer: The pivot point for the board in water

questions.question: What is the most critical factor in tube-riding success?
What is the physics principle behind aerial maneuvers?
questions.answer: Conservation of momentum and angular momentum

questions.question: What is the maximum force G-load in professional surfing turns?
What is the most expensive professional surfboard ever made?
questions.answer: Custom boards by master shapers, thousands of dollars

questions.question: What ancient Hawaiian tradition is still honored in modern surfing culture?
questions.answer: Respect for the ocean and spiritual connection

questions.question: What is the longest documented surfing ride ever?
questions.answer: Over 3 minutes in river tidal bore conditions

questions.question: What is the world's most consistent surf spot?
What is the biggest challenge in cold-water surfing?
questions.answer: Hypothermia and extended duration in cold conditions

questions.question: What is the relationship between tide and wave quality?
questions.answer: Tides affect water depth and wave breaking characteristics

questions.question: What is the atmospheric pressure system that generates the most consistent swells?
What is the most important metric in modern surfboard hydrodynamics?
questions.answer: Wetted surface area and volume distribution

What is the future of surfboard design according to emerging research?
